claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you asking about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm asking about my son Alex. He's 18 and I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to understand his current situation.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, is he attending school, college, sixth form, or an approved training course?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is. He's doing his A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful, thank you. Since Alex is 18 and in approved education, I need to ask a few more questions about your circumstances.

**Do you live with Alex in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. We live together here in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment.

**Are you responsible for Alex - meaning you're supporting him financially with things like food, clothes, or pocket money?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I am. I support him with everything he needs - food, clothes, and his other expenses.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit based on what you've told me.

Based on the information you've provided:
- Alex is 18 years old
- He is in approved education (doing A-levels at sixth form college)
- You live with him in the UK
- You are responsible for him and support him financially

This means you meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex. The key point is that even though Alex is over 16, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and stays in approved education or training - which A-levels at sixth form college definitely counts as.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Your eligibility will continue as long as Alex remains in approved education or training and is under 20 years old. If Alex's circumstances change - for example, if he leaves education, starts working 24 hours or more per week, or starts claiming certain benefits in his own right - your Child Benefit may be affected, so you would need to inform HMRC.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 18 years old and is currently doing A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Under the Child Benefit rules, you can still qualify for Child Benefit if your child is under 20 and they stay in approved education or training. The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for him, providing financial support for his needs including food, clothes, and other expenses. All eligibility criteria are met: the child is under 20, in approved education, the claimant lives with the child in the UK, and the claimant is responsible for the child.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old son Alex. Although Alex is over 16, he remains eligible because he is under 20 and is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form college). The claimant lives with Alex in the UK and is responsible for him, providing financial support. All eligibility requirements are satisfied.
